item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I goddamn love this one, but what do matryoshka dolls have to do with Mayan lower demons? Also, why did they keep popping out in weird reverse order, but the camazotz came last?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The incident log put me off at first, because had it ended before the log, this would have been simply a great little exercise in body horror (especially letting the reader figure out what's happening on their own), while the log went off in a slapsticky (see what I did there) over the top humorous direction. Then I came to my senses and realized that juxtaposition is what pushes this into greatness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Security and medical personnel arrived at this time. Personnel kept their distance from D-963-W's corpse, before Director Adelaide informed them via intercom that D-963-W was the last subject to have been exposed to SCP-3963, and that the danger had subsided.

And then the body exploded as well.

SURPRISE MADAFAKA with a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, I'm confused about the incident log. It says that the rope carrying 3963 snapped and it shattered on impact with the ground. Would that mean that it's neutralized now, or do the pieces of 3963 still keep their anomalous effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because I found that this was a creative and interesting concept. I also liked the test logs a lot, as they did a great job with building mystery.

Only complaint: R. Patel's repeated injuries felt too comical for the piece. Maybe if a lighter tone had been set up before hand I would've loved it, but it felt out of place. Not enough to ruin the piece in any way, but still out of place

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh… I think it's alright as-is? What would the suggested replacement word be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"As the Corpse LAY on the ground"

Typo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fucking incredible

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Matryoshka doll is another name for a Russian nesting doll

[Edit] Wait, or were you talking about "Here comes the boom", because I don't get that reference either

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rewrite notice formatting is borked on mobile. Otherwise, a very good piece of spatio-temporal body-horror. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Feexed what was pointed out, thanks.

ETA: Excluding the bit mentioned in 'Narrative'. I feel like it's ambiguous enough to not fully comprehend what's happening for most.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great concept, and wonderful imagery at the end. However, it's obvious that this piece was rushed and posted without much proofreading. It certainly gives the article a dynamic, punchy tone, but the cardinal sin of this site is posting unfinished work to the mainlist, and there's too much unfinished business here for me to upvote.

Punctuation use: mainly to do with non-standard use or placement of commas, semicolons and dashes. e.g. The man appeared confused, and balked at his surroundings for approximately thirty seconds - after which, he began to scream, and tore at his clothing. I'd drop the comma after "after which", probably go for "he began to scream and tear at his clothing", and replace the dash with a comma.

Tone: mostly good, but for me the pendulum swings too far in the direction of "writing to maximise impact" and away from "writing to perfectly fit scientific tone". I feel a lot of this would be cleared up once the overly suspenseful "… – after which, …" is swept away. Another thing: though it is rarely debilitating. Using "it" to refer to a previously mentioned entity is a tone-breaking bugbear of mine – it seems too informal. Also, as mentioned elsewhere in this thread, some of the wording for Patel's slapstick misfortunes.

Narrative: as written, it was kind of obvious to me with the 'foul-smelling, squishy' etc. places were inside something's digestive tract, which deflated the impact of the reveal later. Maybe I'm the only one scatalogically minded enough to guess it straight away, but I'd consider rewriting the setup to make it more ambiguous, and therefore the climax at the end more surprising.

I'll happily upvote once some of the above issues are fixed up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get what this is supposed to be referencing1, but thanks for the upvote!

Footnotes
1. 'here comes the boom?'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here comes the boom. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Worst. Matryoshka. Ever.

+1

item.title: Spoilerz
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

He was double-crossed by the Camazotz. The creature intended to use the object to possess the human, but it didn't quite work as intended - so he probably spent his time either inside the amulet itself, or inside the Camazotz…depending on whichever you think is cooler.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, where did the Mayan guy come from, before all of those people touched the pendant? What did SCP-3963-A look like to him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, I was pretty mean to him here - he could probably use some downtime in the form of a 3301 testing session.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gaaah. R. Patel better have some PTO coming up. This combined body horror with a genuinely interesting anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While that is probably a better choice for tighter tone, it's a little too clunky, and I prefer its current punchiness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Accidental Camazotz is best Camazotz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Psst: Female archaeologists exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking of the slide that shoves you into the ground for a while, but the end result is so pleasantly fucked up. Gotta feel for Researcher Patel at the end there. I'd probably tone-check that last line; maybe "R. Patel sustained minor injuries during this final event, including loss of eyebrows."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just barely noticed the recurring joke involving Patel getting injured. Pretty brilliant piece of slapstick comedy amidst pretty brilliant body horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The woman produced a male, then the male produced Agent Castillo. So, unless the woman is the other digger, you've missed one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's another person in-between the woman and the agent, they're all accounted for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two members of the dig were lost, but only one came out between the woman and the agent?

Anyways, this is disgusting. Take your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's recursivore with temporal shenanigans fucking fuck I hate it so much

ETA: I also just now noticed how the lettering for the D-Class goes in reverse, for good reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ayyy, first mainlister in forever. Written over the course of a few hours and immediately posted, as I have not had the patience to see any projects through as of late, so I bum-rushed and powered through this idea. It started as an odd twist of SCP-963, hope ya like it.

ETA: If you enjoyed this, more of my stuff can be found here: Shaggydredlocks

ETA 2: Forgot to thank Taffeta for suggesting such an awesome title!
